message:
Ever come home after a truly rotten day — said nothing, kept it together — and your dog takes one look at you and just KNOWS? Comes over, presses close, won't leave your side? 🐾

They're not guessing. Your dog's nose is a superpower: around 300 million scent receptors, compared to our measly 6 million. When you're stressed, your body quietly releases chemicals like cortisol — and your dog can literally smell that shift in you. In one study, dogs picked out the scent of a stressed person with around 90% accuracy. They smell the day you've had before you've said a single word.

So that nudge, that lean, that head resting on your knee when you're low? It's your dog reading you on a level we can barely imagine — and choosing to be right there with you. 💛

When you're having a hard day, what does your dog do? I'd love to hear. 🐶
